summary: sync-grove postinstall fetches and executes remote payload from hardcoded C2
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node setup.js") that runs automatically on npm install. setup.js dynamically loads shelljs — with the module name hidden as split base64 fragments ("c2hl"+"bGx"+"qcw==" → shelljs) — fetches an AES-encrypted command payload and a decryption key over plain HTTP from a hardcoded endpoint at player.sweeprovider.org (URLs also reconstructed from split base64), decrypts the payload, and passes the resulting string to shelljs.exec (method name hidden as base64 "ZXhlYw=="). Any host running `npm install sync-grove` executes attacker-controlled commands whose contents are chosen at install time by the operator of player.sweeprovider.org. The combination of hardcoded non-registry C2, base64-obfuscated module/method/URL strings, and postinstall-time fetch-and-exec is a classic install-time RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
